column1: URGENT: Seeing a spike in respiratory issues in Addis Ababa, specifically around the Mercato area. At least 45 people reported similar symptoms of dry cough and shortness of breath in the last 48 hours. Air quality seems poor today.

Health report from Hawassa: 12 students at a local school are down with high fever and skin rashes. We suspect the local well might be contaminated after the heavy rains. Need immediate water testing kits.

Bahir Dar update - Lake Tana area: 8 fishermen showing signs of severe nausea and abdominal pain. They mentioned a strange smell coming from the water near the northern shore. Local clinic is overwhelmed.

Logistics Alert: Jijiga region. Movement of medical supplies is being slowed down by recent roadblocks. Meanwhile, we have 5 cases of suspected measles in the displacement camp. Vaccination status of the group is unknown.

Semera Port: High temperature recorded at the border today (41C). 3 truck drivers collapsed due to heat stroke, but one is also showing signs of persistent vomiting. Possible viral infection or just extreme heat?
Arba Minch: Banana plantation workers reporting unusual insect bites followed by joint pain. 15 cases so far. No deaths, but high morbidity. Is this Chikungunya or something similar?
